Title: CRISPR/Cas knockout of the NADPH oxidase gene reduces ROS overaccumulation and enhances OsRbohB heat stress tolerance in rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Respiratory burst oxidase homolog (plant NADPH oxidase)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heat stress inducible; previously reported as the highest-expressed OsRboh family member under heat stress at different rice growth stages; measured in seedlings and grains under long-term heat stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout reduces ROS overaccumulation and improves heat stress tolerance, grain yield, grain quality, and starch accumulation under long-term heat stress; overexpression causes opposite effects
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas knockout of OsRbohB significantly reduced O2•− and H2O2 accumulation in seeds, seedlings, anthers and grains under heat stress. 2. OsRbohB knockout increased heat tolerance traits including lower membrane injury and leaf withering, higher chlorophyll and relative water content, and stronger antioxidant enzyme activities during prolonged heat stress. 3. OsRbohB knockout improved grain filling-related traits, starch/amylose/amylopectin content, milling and appearance quality, and increased grain yield under reproductive-stage heat stress, whereas overexpression largely caused opposite phenotypes. 4. OsRbohB knockout upregulated heat shock-related genes OsHSP23.7, OsHSP17.7, OsHSF7 and OsHsfA2a during prolonged heat stress.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression under ubiquitin promoter; Southern blot; qRT-PCR; ROS quantification; antioxidant and starch-synthesis enzyme activity assays; growth/yield/quality phenotyping under staged heat stress
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-term heat stress at germination (40°C), seedling stage (40°C or 43°C daytime), and reproductive stage (38°C daytime during defined post-heading windows)
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsHSP23.7; OsHSP17.7; OsHSF7; OsHsfA2a (expression-associated downstream heat shock-related genes)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Under unstressed conditions knockout slightly inhibited seed germination and produced slightly shorter plants, consistent with a requirement for appropriate basal ROS in normal growth
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS act as stress signaling molecules, but overaccumulation causes oxidative damage, membrane injury, impaired floral development, reduced starch synthesis, and yield/quality loss under heat stress
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene rather than Pathway Gene because the study focuses on ROS production and heat-stress-associated metabolic/physiological regulation rather than a nutrient biosynthetic enzyme; authors identify OsRbohB as a key negative regulator of heat stress tolerance and propose genome editing or chemical inhibition as applications
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for breeding heat-tolerant rice by knockout/editing or targeted mutation to reduce ROS overaccumulation and improve yield and grain quality under warming conditions; also proposed target for chemical inhibitor development
